---
title: "LegendPlex & Luminex Assays: Correlations"
output: 
  html_document:
    toc: true            # Enable Table of Contents
    toc_float: true      # Makes the Table of Contents float (optional)
date: "10-09-2025"
---


# Function to install/load packages:
```{r load_packages function, include=FALSE}
load_packages <- function(packages) {
  sapply(packages, function(pkg) {
    if (!require(pkg, character.only = TRUE)) {
      install.packages(pkg, dependencies = TRUE)
      library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
    }
  })
}

packages <- c("purrr", "dplyr", "tidyr", "knitr", "magrittr", "reshape2", 
              "tidyverse", "ggplot2", "RColorBrewer", "stringr",
              "rstatix", "ggpubr", "ggsignif", "FSA",  
              "readxl", "viridis", "patchwork",  "readr",
              "ggfortify", "factoextra", "pheatmap",
              "cowplot","pheatmap","psych","cowplot")

load_packages(packages)
library(gt)
getwd()

```

# Import data:
```{r Import data}
df <- read_excel("02-LegendPlex_data.xlsx")

# change names of sample types for consistency 
df <- df %>%  
 mutate(Sample_Type = case_when(
    Sample_Type == "Mid-turbinate swab" ~ "MTS",
    Sample_Type == "Nasopharyngeal swab" ~ "NPS",
    Sample_Type == "Oropharyngeal swab" ~ "OPS",
    TRUE ~ Sample_Type
  ))


#Convert columns as factors:
my_dataframe <- df %>%
  convert_as_factor(Analytes, Sampling_Method) %>% 
  group_by(ID, Sample_Type, Analytes) %>% 
  # Calculate the mean for nostrils (Left and Right) for professional samples only
  mutate(mean_concentration = ifelse(Sample_Type %in% c("MLF", "MTS") & !is.na(Nostril), mean(concentration.est, na.rm = TRUE),  # Mean for Left/Right nostrils
                                     concentration.est)) %>%  # Keep other samples as they are
  ungroup() 
  
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# 1. Keep only participants with all sample types
# all sample types present in dataset
all_types <- unique(my_dataframe$Sample_Type)

# keep only participants that have all sample types
df_filtered <- my_dataframe %>% filter(Sampling_Strategy == "Professional") %>% 
  group_by(ID) %>%
  filter(all(all_types %in% Sample_Type)) %>%
  ungroup()

# count how many samples per sample type there are
df_filtered %>%
  distinct(SampleID, Sample_Type) %>%
  count(Sample_Type)

# create new column by combining participant ID and sample type
df_filtered <- df_filtered %>% mutate(ID_Site = paste0(ID,"_",Sample_Type))

df_filtered2 <- df_filtered %>%
  distinct(Analytes, ID_Site, .keep_all = TRUE)

df_filtered2 %>%
  distinct(SampleID, Sample_Type) %>%
  count(Sample_Type)
# convert to wide format
wide <- df_filtered2 %>% dplyr::select(ID, Sample_Type, mean_concentration, Analytes) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = Analytes, values_from = mean_concentration)
```



# Import total antibody concentration data
```{r}
abs<-  read_excel("01-Luminex_Total_Abs_Data.xlsx", 
    col_types = c("text", "text", "numeric", 
        "numeric", "numeric", "numeric", 
        "numeric", "text", "text", "text", 
        "text", "text", "text", "text", "text", 
        "text", "numeric", "numeric", "numeric", 
        "text", "numeric"))
# Function to replace "OOR <" values with half of the minimum or half of LLOQ if all values are NA
replace_missing_with_min <- function(x, lloq) {
  min_val <- min(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.finite(min_val)) {
    return((1/2) * min_val)
  } else {
    return((1/2) * lloq)  # Use half of LLOQ if all values are NA
  }
}

# Function to replace "OOR >" values with the maximum x2 concentration in range
replace_missing_with_max <- function(x) {
  max_val <- max(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.finite(max_val)) {
    return(max_val*2)
  } else {
    return(NA)  # If all values are NA, return NA or another desired fallback
  }
}

# Apply the functions per isotype and sample type
abs <- abs %>%
  group_by(Analyte, Sample_Type) %>%
  mutate(
    concentration = case_when(
      is.na(Conc_in_Range) & Notes == "OOR <" ~ replace_missing_with_min(Conc_in_Range, LLOQ),
      is.na(Conc_in_Range) & Notes == "OOR >" ~ replace_missing_with_max(Conc_in_Range),
      TRUE ~ Conc_in_Range
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

#  Calculate log10 of the new concentration column
abs <- abs %>%
  mutate(log10.new = log10(concentration))


abs <- abs %>%
  group_by(ID, Sample_Type) %>%
  mutate(
    mean_concentration = ifelse(
      n() > 1,
      mean(concentration, na.rm = TRUE),
      concentration
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()


# keep only participants that have all sample types
ab_filtered <- abs %>% filter(Sampling_Strategy == "Professional") %>% 
  group_by(ID) %>%
  filter(all(all_types %in% Sample_Type)) %>%
  ungroup()

ab_filtered %>%
  distinct(Description, Sample_Type) %>%
  count(Sample_Type)

ab_filtered <- ab_filtered %>% mutate(ID_Site = paste0(ID,"_",Sample_Type))

ab_filtered2 <- ab_filtered %>%
  distinct(Analyte, ID_Site, .keep_all = TRUE)

ab_filtered2 %>%
  distinct(Description, Sample_Type) %>%
  count(Sample_Type)

ab_filtered2 %>%
  distinct(ID, Sample_Type) %>%
  count(Sample_Type)

wide_ab <- ab_filtered2 %>% dplyr::select(ID, Sample_Type, mean_concentration, Analyte) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = Analyte, values_from = mean_concentration)

wide_ab <- wide_ab %>% filter(!is.na(IgM))

table(wide_ab$Sample_Type)

# get IDs that have all 5 sample types
ids_with_all <- wide_ab %>%
  distinct(ID, Sample_Type) %>%
  count(ID) %>%
  filter(n == 5) %>%
  pull(ID)

# filter the main dataset to only those IDs
wide_ab_balanced <- wide_ab %>%
  filter(ID %in% ids_with_all)

# check
table(wide_ab_balanced$Sample_Type)

wide_ab_balanced <- wide_ab_balanced %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~log10(.)))

wide_10 <- wide %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~log10(.)))

wide_ab_balanced <- wide_ab_balanced %>%
  mutate(ID_Type = paste(ID, Sample_Type, sep = "_"))

wide_10 <- wide_10 %>%
  mutate(ID_Type = paste(ID, Sample_Type, sep = "_"))

wide_combined <- wide_ab_balanced %>%
  inner_join(wide_10, by = "ID_Type", suffix = c("_ab", "_cyto"))


num_df <- wide_combined %>%
  # drop obvious metadata first
  select(-ID_ab, -Sample_Type_ab, -ID_Type, -ID_cyto, -Sample_Type_cyto) %>%
  # keep only numeric analytes (handles spaces/greek chars in names)
  select(where(is.numeric))

# (optional) log if not already logged
# num_df <- num_df %>% mutate(across(everything(), ~log10(. + 1)))

cors2 <- cor(num_df, method = "spearman", use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
```

# Spearman co-correlations
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)

# all analyte columns = drop IDs and sample type cols
analytes <- setdiff(names(wide_combined),
                    c("ID_ab", "Sample_Type_ab", "ID_Type",
                      "ID_cyto", "Sample_Type_cyto"))

# function is the same as before
cor_one <- function(analyte) {
  df <- wide_combined %>%
    select(ID_ab, Sample_Type_ab, !!sym(analyte)) %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Sample_Type_ab, values_from = !!sym(analyte))
  
  df_complete <- df %>% drop_na()
  if (nrow(df_complete) < 3) return(NULL)
  
  mat <- df_complete %>% select(-ID_ab)
  combs <- combn(names(mat), 2, simplify = FALSE)
  
  map_dfr(combs, function(xy) {
    ct <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(mat[[xy[1]]], mat[[xy[2]]],
                                    method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
    tibble(
      Analyte = analyte,
      Sample_Type1 = xy[1],
      Sample_Type2 = xy[2],
      rho = unname(ct$estimate),
      n = sum(complete.cases(mat[, xy])),
      p_value = ct$p.value
    )
  })
}

# run across all analytes (antibodies + cytokines)
cor_long <- map_dfr(analytes, cor_one)

cor_long <- cor_long %>%
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "fdr"))
```

# Visualize the correlations
```{r}

cor_plot <- cor_long %>%
  mutate(
    Pair = paste(Sample_Type1, Sample_Type2, sep = " vs "),
    significance = case_when(
      p_adj < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_adj < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_adj < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ ""
    ),
    Group = ifelse(Analyte %in% c("IgA", "IgG", "IgM"), "Total\n Antibodies", "Cytokines")
  )

cor <- ggplot(cor_plot, aes(x = Pair, y = Analyte, fill = rho)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = significance), color = "black", size = 3) +
  facet_grid(Group ~ ., scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +  # antibodies vs cytokines
  scale_fill_distiller(palette = "BrBG", limits = c(-1, 1), na.value = "grey90") +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL, fill = "rho") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 16), strip.text = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")  # facet title size
  )
cor
# Save plot:
#ggsave("Abs_Cyto_cor_heatmap.tiff", width = 10, height = 8.8, dpi = 300, compression = "lzw")

```


# make the data
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(ggplot2)

# --- prepare data ---
# split antibody and cytokine data
ab_data <- wide_combined %>%
  select(ID_ab, Sample_Type_ab, IgA, IgG, IgM) %>%
  rename(ID = ID_ab, Sample_Type = Sample_Type_ab)

cyto_data <- wide_combined %>%
  select(ID_cyto, Sample_Type_cyto, APRIL:Perforin) %>%
  rename(ID = ID_cyto, Sample_Type = Sample_Type_cyto)

# merge by ID + Sample_Type (keeps only matched samples)
merged_data <- inner_join(ab_data, cyto_data, by = c("ID","Sample_Type"))


```


# Fig. 4 co-correlations
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(Hmisc)
# ---- your functional groups in the exact order you want on axes ----
group_order <- list(
  `Antiviral/Cytotoxic`     = c("Perforin","Granzyme A","IFN-α2","IFN-λ1","IFN-γ"),
  `Chemokine` = c("CXCL10","CCL4","CCL2","CXCL8","CXCL1"),
  `Humoral/B Cell`          = c("IgM","IgG","IgA","APRIL"),
   `Inflammatory`            = c("IL-33","IL-6")
)
ordered_analytes <- unlist(group_order, use.names = FALSE)

# ---- (optional) map dataset column names -> your display labels ----
# If your data uses names like IFNA2, IFNG, GranzymeA, IL6/IL33, map them:
synonyms <- c(
  "GranzymeA"="Granzyme A",
  "IFNA2"="IFN-α2",
  "IFNL1"="IFN-λ1",
  "IFNG" ="IFN-γ",
  "IL6"  ="IL-6",
  "IL33" ="IL-33"
)

# --- custom colors for each group ---
group_colors <- c(
  "Antiviral/Cytotoxic"     = "#B24745",  # red
  "Chemokine" = "#79AF97",  # orange
  "Humoral/B Cell"          = "#8491B4", # dark blue
  "Inflammatory"            = "#EDC948"  # blue

)

# ---- helper: compute upper-triangle correlations (per Sample_Type) ----
get_corr_df <- function(df, stype) {
  dat <- df %>%
    filter(Sample_Type == stype) %>%
    select(-ID, -Sample_Type) %>%
    select(where(is.numeric))

  # rename columns to display labels if synonyms provided
  nm <- colnames(dat)
  nm2 <- ifelse(nm %in% names(synonyms), unname(synonyms[nm]), nm)
  colnames(dat) <- nm2

  # keep only analytes you care about, in your order
  keep <- intersect(ordered_analytes, colnames(dat))
  if (length(keep) < 2) return(NULL)
  dat <- dat[, keep, drop = FALSE]

  # Spearman r + p
  rc   <- rcorr(as.matrix(dat), type = "spearman")
  rmat <- rc$r
  pmat <- rc$P

  # upper triangle (no diag)
  upper_idx <- upper.tri(rmat, diag = FALSE)

  # long-form, one row per unique pair
  tibble(
    Var1 = rownames(rmat)[row(rmat)[upper_idx]],
    Var2 = colnames(rmat)[col(rmat)[upper_idx]],
    rho  = rmat[upper_idx],
    pval = pmat[upper_idx],
    Sample_Type = stype
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      padj = p.adjust(pval, method = "fdr"),
      stars = case_when(
        padj <= 0.001 ~ "***",
        padj <= 0.01  ~ "**",
        padj <= 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      ),
      # lock the order on both axes to your group order
      Var1 = factor(Var1, levels = ordered_analytes),
      Var2 = factor(Var2, levels = ordered_analytes)
    )
}

# ---- compute all sample types ----
all_corrs <- map_dfr(unique(merged_data$Sample_Type),
                     ~ get_corr_df(merged_data, .x))

# ---- palette: BrBG reversed (brown = pos, green = neg) ----
pal_brbg <- rev(brewer.pal(11, "BrBG"))

# ---- (optional) group separators (lines between blocks) ----
present <- ordered_analytes[ordered_analytes %in% (levels(all_corrs$Var1) %||% ordered_analytes)]
grp_lens <- lengths(lapply(group_order, function(v) intersect(v, present)))
cuts <- cumsum(grp_lens); cuts <- cuts[cuts < length(present)]

# ---- plot (upper triangle only) ----
p <- ggplot(all_corrs, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = rho)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  geom_text(aes(label = stars), size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = pal_brbg, limits = c(-1, 1), na.value = "white") +
  scale_x_discrete(drop = FALSE, limits = present) +
  scale_y_discrete(drop = FALSE, limits = present) +
  facet_wrap(~ Sample_Type) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust =0.5, size=13.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14),
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.3), 
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 18)
  ) +
  labs(fill = "rho", x=NULL, y=NULL,
       title = NULL)

# add separators if you want them
if (length(cuts)) {
  for (k in cuts) {
    p <- p +
      geom_hline(yintercept = k + 0.5, linewidth = 0.3, color = "grey30") +
      geom_vline(xintercept = k + 0.5, linewidth = 0.3, color = "grey30")
  }
}



library(ggnewscale)

# group block positions
grp_df <- tibble(
  group = names(group_order),
  size = lengths(group_order)
) %>%
  mutate(
    start = lag(cumsum(size), default = 0) + 0.5,
    end   = cumsum(size) + 0.5
  )

# strips for y-axis (left side)
axis_rects_y <- grp_df %>%
  transmute(
    xmin = 0.5 - 0.3,   # slightly to the left of the first column
    xmax = 0.5, 
    ymin = start,
    ymax = end,
    Group = group
  )

# strips for x-axis (top side, above tiles)
axis_rects_x <- grp_df %>%
  transmute(
    xmin = start,
    xmax = end,
    ymin = max(end) + 0.5,         # just above the last row
    ymax = max(end) + 0.8, 
    Group = group
  )

axis_rects <- bind_rows(axis_rects_x, axis_rects_y)

# extend plot limits so top strip is visible
gg<- p +
  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +
  geom_rect(data = axis_rects,
            aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = ymin, ymax = ymax, fill = Group),
            inherit.aes = FALSE, color = NA) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")   # allows drawing outside normal panel

# Visualize plot:
gg
# Save as TIFF
ggsave("cross_correlations.tiff", gg,
       width = 13.9, height = 9, units = "in",
       dpi = 600, device = "tiff", bg = "white", compression = "lzw")
```
